exam_name: Microsoft Dynamics 365 Finance and Operations Apps Developer
certification_name: Microsoft Dynamics 365: Finance and Operations Apps Developer

testing_centers.notes: Available at Certiport Authorized Testing Centers (primarily academic)

id_requirements: One valid, government-issued photo ID. Name on ID must match registration. For online: webcam required, room must be clear of people and materials.
exam_rules: No notes, books, or study materials. No phones or smartwatches. Whiteboard or scratch paper may be provided by testing center. Online: no dual monitors, no virtual machines, no headphones, must be alone in a closed room.
renewal_options: Microsoft certifications (Associate/Expert/Specialty) are renewed annually for free via a short renewal assessment on Microsoft Learn. Renewal assessment available 6 months before expiry. Fundamentals certifications do not expire.
target_audience: Developers who work with finance and operations apps in Microsoft Dynamics 365 to implement and extend applications that meet the requirements of a business
recommended_experience: Deep knowledge and experience using the underlying framework, data structures, and objects associated with the finance and operations solutions. Experience with products and services that include Microsoft Visual Studio, Azure, Power Platform, Azure DevOps, Lifecycle Services tools, Power Platform admin center, Implementation portal, Copilot, Postman, Microsoft 365, reporting tools (for example, SQL Server Reporting Services [SSRS], Power BI), and SQL Server Management Studio

prerequisites.description: Experience with products and services that include Microsoft Visual Studio, Azure, Power Platform, Azure DevOps, Lifecycle Services tools, Power Platform admin center, Implementation portal, Copilot, Postman, Microsoft 365, reporting tools (for example, SQL Server Reporting Services [SSRS], Power BI), and SQL Server Management Studio

retake_policy.notes: No waiting period for first retake if score >= 500 on failed attempt. If score < 500: 14-day waiting period before retake. Maximum 5 attempts per exam per year (365 days). Free retake voucher sometimes included in official instructor-led training.

domains.name: Plan the architecture and solution design

objectives.title: Plan for the major components of finance and operations apps
sub_objectives: Differentiate between the cloud and on-premises versions of finance and operations apps
Extend the functionality of finance and operations apps into the Microsoft ecosystem
Implement application lifecycle management (ALM)
Manage environments by using Lifecycle Services tools and components
Research and resolve issues by using Issue Search
Perform activities that require asset libraries
Manage package deployments between environments
Manage Unified Developer Experience (UDE) developer environments in Power Platform admin center
Manage environments by using the Implementation portal

objectives.title: Customize finance and operations apps by using Visual Studio
sub_objectives: Create and manage extension models
Manage metadata by using Application Explorer
Synchronize data dictionary changes with the application database
Create elements by using element designers
Perform debugging activities
Manage source code and artifacts by using Microsoft Azure DevOps version control
Create, check out, review, and check in code
Maintain project artifacts
Branch and merge code
Compare code and resolve version conflicts
Choose a version control system
Implement continuous integration and continuous delivery (CI/CD)

objectives.title: Create and extend UI elements
sub_objectives: Create and extend forms
Create and extend menus and menu items
Create and use label files

objectives.title: Create and extend the data model
sub_objectives: Create and extend tables
Create and extend views, queries, and maps
Create and extend data entities

objectives.title: Create classes and extend AOT elements
sub_objectives: Create and extend classes
Add event handler methods to a class
Add attributes or modifiers to a class

objectives.title: Develop X++ code
sub_objectives: Implement common structured programming constructs of X++
Create, read, update, and delete (CRUD) data
Implement global functions in X++
Resolve best practice warnings
Develop object-oriented code
Implement variable scoping
Implement inheritance and abstraction concept
Implement query objects and the QueryBuilder class
Implement attribute classes
Implement Chain of Command (CoC)
Assess the extensibility of code and avoid breaking changes
Implement event handler classes and delegates
Implement finance and operations app framework functionality
Implement the SysOperation framework
Implement the workflow framework
Implement the SysExtensionSerializer framework
Perform testing

Run unit tests in Test Explorer

objectives.title: Select reporting tools in finance and operations apps
sub_objectives: Create and modify report data sources and supporting classes
Implement reporting security requirements

Create, modify, and deploy reports in finance and operations apps by using SSRS
Create and modify finance and operations apps reports by using Power BI
Create and modify finance and operations apps reports by using Microsoft Excel
Create and modify finance and operations apps reports by using Electronic reporting

objectives.title: Evaluate data integration patterns and scenarios

Select an appropriate data integration API

Implement data integration concepts and solutions
Consume external web services by using RESTful APIs and Simple Object Access Protocol (SOAP)
Implement Electronic reporting integrations
Implement custom services

Implement data management
